category_title: Students who experience enhanced understanding and focused interest during mental health disclosures
minimal_context: The student is participating in a classroom discussion to evaluate personal beliefs about mental health based on peer disclosures.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's take some time to evaluate our personal beliefs based on the mental health disclosures shared by your peers in class today.

STUDENT: (Listens intently) I have an increased interest in the topic and I am making sure to watch my language.

TUTOR: What are your thoughts on the disclosures that were just shared?

STUDENT: Hearing these mental health self-disclosures is bringing up some of the myths and mental health concerns I have.

TUTOR: Can you explain what you mean by that?

STUDENT: Well, it causes me discomfort. Because of the myths I have, when hearing mental health disclosures, I equate mental illness with a propensity for violence.

options.A: Identify when the self-disclosures contain erroneous information and use the opportunity to educate the class as a whole.
options.B: Shift the focus of the discussion to a progressive and methodical tuning of awareness to each body part through a body scan.
options.C: Use the journalist method, speaking in short sentences like newspaper headlines to talk briefly and concisely about the main details.
options.D: Advise the student to take a short break of 5-15 minutes after every hour of the discussion and a longer break after two hours.
